input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen on Android. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` no longer works in .NET 10 (which aligns with Android deprecating `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE`).

This is a knowledge question — I don't need to search a codebase. Let me provide a concise answer.

In .NET MAUI / Android, `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` was deprecated in Android API 30. Starting with .NET 10 (and newer Android targets), the recommended approach is to use **Edge-to-Edge** mode and the `WindowInsets` API to handle keyboard (IME) visibility.
 
In .NET 10, MAUI switched to edge-to-edge rendering by default on Android, which fundamentally changed how soft input adjustment works. The old `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was a MAUI wrapper around the deprecated Android `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE`, so the real solution involves working with the new insets system rather than relying on the old resize behavior. The deprecated `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` behavior is being replaced by the `WindowInsetsCompat` API with IME type handling. For MAUI 10 specifically, since edge-to-edge is now the default, I need to ensure the layout properly responds to window insets rather than relying on the old soft input mode adjustments—the most practical approach is setting `windowSoftInputMode="adjustResize"` in the manifest as a fallback, or using the newer `WindowInsets` API through platform-specific code to handle IME insets directly.

Setting up the window insets listener... For MAUI 10, edge-to-edge is the default behavior, and `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` has been removed, so I need to ensure the AndroidManifest.xml has `adjustResize` set and either use `ViewCompat.SetOnApplyWindowInsetsListener` to handle IME insets manually or fall back to `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Pan` which still works but pans the view instead of resizing.
